task.title: Add scoped change claims for parallel work
task.summary: Implement first-class temporary change claims so parallel sessions can coordinate ownership across documentation, roadmap, builds, validation, and code scopes, not only roadmap tasks.

goal.outcome: Parallel CodeWiki sessions can claim arbitrary change scopes and see cross-layer overlap warnings/conflicts before making semantic changes.
acceptance: A first-class claim/lease model exists for session-owned change scopes with optional roadmap task/build anchors.
Claims support layers for knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, validation, and graph/source paths.
A Pi-facing tool can create, release, heartbeat, and list active claims without editing generated graph files directly.
Generated state/index derives active claims and overlap conflicts, distinguishing read/read, read/write warning, and write/write conflict.
Status/graph surfaces active claim count, conflicts, and per-session claim details alongside existing task-session parallel state.
Docs and skills clarify roadmap tasks remain work truth while claims are temporary concurrency leases, not source of truth.
Smoke coverage proves claim creation, release/expiry, and overlap detection.
Roadmap id generation does not reuse archived task ids.
non_goals: Do not implement distributed cross-machine locks in this first slice.
Do not make claims replace roadmap tasks, compiler builds, validation reports, git, or code review.
Do not block every write automatically before explicit claim enforcement exists.
verification: Run direct current-source graph rebuild and inspect claims view.
Run codewiki_state refresh=false after current-source rebuild.

summary: Implemented first-class scoped change claims for parallel CodeWiki work. Added `.codewiki/claims.json` lease persistence, claim normalization/conflict detection, `codewiki_claim` Pi tool, graph/status claim views, state summary exposure, smoke coverage, docs/skills, and archived task id reuse guard.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: A first-class claim/lease model exists for session-owned change scopes with optional roadmap task/build anchors.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: ChangeClaim* domain types and application/claims.ts records id, session_id, agent_name, mode, scopes, optional task_id/build_ref, TTL, status, release/expiry.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Claims support layers for knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, validation, and graph/source paths.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: CHANGE_CLAIM_LAYER_VALUES and schema support knowledge, roadmap, code, build, validation, graph, and source.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: A Pi-facing tool can create, release, heartbeat, and list active claims without editing generated graph files directly.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: codewiki_claim tool and executeCodewikiClaim call mutateChangeClaims; graph/status rebuild follows mutation.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Generated state/index derives active claims and overlap conflicts, distinguishing read/read, read/write warning, and write/write conflict.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: detectClaimConflicts implements read/write warning and write/write conflict; smoke asserts warning and default block.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Status/graph surfaces active claim count, conflicts, and per-session claim details alongside existing task-session parallel state.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: buildStatusState adds claim fields under parallel; graph views.claims exposes counts, claims, conflicts; codewiki_state exposes claims.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Docs and skills clarify roadmap tasks remain work truth while claims are temporary concurrency leases, not source of truth.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Updated lexicon, API, roadmap, graph, overview, adapters, README, and CodeWiki skills.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Smoke coverage proves claim creation, release/expiry, and overlap detection.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: scripts/smoke-test.mjs claim smoke covers create, warning, block, release, expiry, and state exposure.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Roadmap id generation does not reuse archived task ids.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: nextTaskIdSequenceForProject includes archive ids; TASK-052 manually corrected after discovering archived TASK-051 reuse.

risks: Pi must reload before the new codewiki_claim tool and claims include schema are available in live tools.
First slice is local-file coordination, not distributed cross-machine locking.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: feat(claims): coordinate parallel work with scoped change leases
commit.body: Add first-class scoped change claims for docs, roadmap, builds, validation, graph/source refs, and code. Claims live in .codewiki/claims.json, expire/release, surface through graph/status/state, and are managed by codewiki_claim. Add smoke coverage and prevent archived task id reuse.

pr.title: feat(claims): coordinate parallel work with scoped change leases
pr.body: Add first-class scoped change claims for docs, roadmap, builds, validation, graph/source refs, and code. Claims live in .codewiki/claims.json, expire/release, surface through graph/status/state, and are managed by codewiki_claim. Add smoke coverage and prevent archived task id reuse.

validation_verdict.rationale: Scoped claims implementation aligns docs, roadmap, source, graph/state, smoke tests, and accepted feedback.
